description: ASE Skill Benchmark — 50 English prompts for comprehensive code generation testing

tasks.prompt: Create a Cu FCC bulk and generate a 2x2x2 supercell. Print the cell info and number of atoms.
Create a Pt(111) 4-layer slab and adsorb a CO molecule on the ontop site. Set vacuum to 10 angstroms. Print the number of atoms in the final structure.
Create a MoS2 monolayer structure. Add 10 angstroms of vacuum and print the cell size.
Optimize an H2O molecule using the EMT calculator. Use the BFGS optimizer and print the energy before and after optimization.
Find the equilibrium lattice constant of Cu FCC bulk using Equation of State (EOS) fitting. Print the equilibrium volume and bulk modulus.
Run 300K Langevin MD for 100 steps on a Cu FCC bulk 2x2x2 supercell. Use a timestep of 5 fs. Print the initial/final temperature and energy.
Run NVE (VelocityVerlet) MD for 50 steps on a Cu FCC bulk. Set the initial temperature to 300K and print the initial/final total energy (kinetic+potential) to verify energy conservation.
Calculate the vibrational frequencies of an N2 molecule. Use the EMT calculator and print the vibrational frequency values.
Save a Cu FCC bulk in VASP POSCAR format and read it back. Print the number of atoms and cell parameters.
Create a Cu Octahedron nanoparticle with length=5. Print the number of atoms and the positions shape.
Create an Al BCC structure using bulk with a lattice constant of 3.3 angstroms. Set cubic=True and print the cell and chemical formula.
Create a Ti HCP bulk structure. Set a=2.95 and c/a=1.59. Print the cell vectors and atomic positions.
Create a Si bulk with diamond structure. The lattice constant is 5.43 angstroms. Make a 3x3x3 supercell and print the number of atoms and cell volume.
Create an NaCl crystal structure using spacegroup 225 (Fm-3m). Place Na at (0,0,0) and Cl at (0.5,0.5,0.5) with a lattice constant of 5.64 angstroms. Print the number of atoms and chemical symbols.
Create a Cu(100) surface with 3 layers. Set size=(3,3,3) and vacuum=12 angstroms. Print the number of atoms and cell info.
Create an Fe BCC(110) surface with 4 layers. Set size=(2,2,4) and vacuum=10 angstroms. Print the number of atoms and cell size.
Cut a general Miller index (2,1,1) surface from Cu bulk with 3 layers. Use the surface() function and add 10 angstroms of vacuum. Print the number of atoms and cell.
Retrieve a CH4 molecule from the ASE G2 database. Print the atomic coordinates, bond lengths, and chemical formula.

Manually create a CO2 molecule as an Atoms object. Place C at the origin and O atoms at 1.16 angstroms on each side. Set the cell to a 10x10x10 box with pbc=False. Calculate and print the interatomic distances using get_distances.
Create a (6,6) carbon nanotube with length=4. Print the number of atoms and cell info.
Create an Au Icosahedron nanoparticle with noshells=3. Print the number of atoms and center of mass.

Create an Al(111) 3-layer slab and adsorb an N2 molecule on the bridge site at a height of 2.0 angstroms. Set vacuum to 10 angstroms. Get N2 using molecule(). Print the number of atoms and atom types in the final structure.

Create a Pt(111) 3-layer slab and adsorb an OH molecule on ontop, bridge, and fcc hollow sites respectively. Attach an EMT calculator to each structure and compute single-point energies. Compare and print which site has the lowest energy.
Optimize an Au FCC bulk structure using EMT with the LBFGS optimizer. Set fmax=0.01 and print the number of optimization steps and final energy.

Simultaneously optimize the lattice constant and atomic positions of a Cu FCC bulk. Use FrechetCellFilter with the BFGS optimizer and converge to fmax=0.01. Print the cell size and energy before and after optimization.
Optimize a Ni FCC bulk using PreconLBFGS. Use the EMT calculator with precon='auto'. Converge to fmax=0.01 and print the number of steps, final energy, and cell parameters.
Run 500K NVT MD on an Ag FCC 2x2x2 supercell using the Bussi thermostat for 200 steps. Use a timestep of 5 fs. Record and print the temperature every 50 steps.
Implement a temperature ramp from 300K to 600K using Langevin MD on a Cu FCC 2x2x2 supercell. Run for 200 steps with a timestep of 5 fs. Print the current temperature every 50 steps.

Run VelocityVerlet NVE MD for 200 steps on a Pd FCC 2x2x2 supercell with an initial temperature of 500K. Use a timestep of 2 fs. Print the difference in total energy (kinetic+potential) between start and end to verify energy conservation.
Run NPT MD on a Cu FCC 3x3x3 supercell using NPTBerendsen at 300K and 1 bar for 200 steps. Use timestep=5 fs, taut=100*units.fs, taup=1000*units.fs. Print the initial/final cell volume and pressure.
Run NPT MD on an Al FCC 2x2x2 supercell using NPTBerendsen at 500K and 10 GPa high pressure for 100 steps. Convert the pressure unit from GPa to eV/Ang^3. Print the initial/final cell volume.
Calculate the vibrational modes of an H2O molecule. Use the EMT calculator and print each vibrational mode frequency in cm^-1 and energy in eV.
Calculate the vibrational frequencies of a CH4 molecule using the EMT calculator. First optimize the structure, then perform the vibration calculation. Filter and print only the real frequencies.

Calculate the diffusion path of a Cu adatom moving from an fcc hollow to an hcp hollow site on a Cu FCC(111) surface using NEB. Use 5 images with IDPP interpolation. Print the energy barrier (difference between maximum energy and initial energy).

Perform a simple NEB calculation where a third Al atom moves between two fixed Al atoms. Use the EMT calculator. Manually create initial and final states as Atoms objects, use 3 images with linear interpolation. Print the energy of each image.

Perform EOS fitting for Ag FCC bulk. Vary the lattice constant over 7 points within a +/-5% range and calculate energies. Fit with Birch-Murnaghan EOS and print the equilibrium lattice constant and bulk modulus in GPa.

Calculate the vibrational frequencies of an N2 molecule using EMT, then use IdealGasThermo to compute the Gibbs free energy at 298.15K and 1 atm. Set geometry='linear' and symmetrynumber=2. Print the result.
Calculate the vibrational frequencies of Cu bulk and use HarmonicThermo to compute the Helmholtz free energy at 300K. Use the EMT calculator. Print the result in eV.
Save an Au FCC bulk to an XYZ format file and read it back. Print the atom types and positions.
Save an NaCl crystal structure in CIF format and read it back. Print the spacegroup information and number of atoms.

Attach an EMT calculator to a Cu FCC bulk and run MD for 10 steps while saving to a trajectory file. Then read the trajectory back and print the total number of frames and the energy of the last frame.
Create an ASE database and store Cu bulk, Ag bulk, and Au bulk structures along with their EMT energies. Then query all entries using db.select() and print each entry's formula and energy.

Store Cu slab structures with different layer counts (2, 3, 4 layers) in an ASE database. Include the layers count as a key-value pair. Use select() to retrieve only the structure with layers=3 and print its number of atoms.

Fix the bottom 2 layers of a Cu(111) 4-layer slab using FixAtoms based on tags. Run BFGS structural optimization with EMT. Compare and print the coordinates of fixed atoms before and after optimization to verify they did not move.

Apply a FixBondLength constraint to an H2 molecule to fix the bond length at 0.9 angstroms. Calculate the energy using EMT and print the bond length and energy before and after applying the constraint.

Adsorb CO on a Pt(111) 3-layer slab. Fix the bottom layer with FixAtoms and fix the C-O bond with FixBondLength. Apply both constraints simultaneously and optimize with BFGS to fmax=0.05. Print the final energy and C-O distance.
Build a NeighborList for a Cu FCC 3x3x3 supercell using natural_cutoffs. Calculate the number of neighbors for each atom and print the average coordination number.

Use get_distances to calculate the distances between atom 0 and all other atoms in a Cu FCC bulk 2x2x2 supercell. Set mic=True to account for periodic boundary conditions. Print the minimum and maximum distances.

Find the equilibrium lattice constant of Cu FCC bulk using EOS, then create a (111) 4-layer slab with that lattice constant. Attach EMT, fix the bottom 2 layers, and run BFGS surface relaxation. Print the final energy and average z-coordinate for each layer.

For Cu, Ag, and Au, create FCC bulk structures and perform EOS fitting with EMT for each metal. Calculate and print the equilibrium lattice constant and bulk modulus for each in a comparison table format.
